subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Gravel Processing Plant G(t) — Rate Interpretation, Total Arrival, Increasing/Decreasing & Max
stimulus: On a certain workday, the rate, in tons per hour, at which unprocessed gravel arrives at a gravel processing plant is modeled by $G(t) = 90 + 45\cos\left(\frac{t^2}{18}\right)$, where $t$ is measured in hours and $0 \le t \le 8$. At the beginning of the workday ($t = 0$), the plant has 500 tons of unprocessed gravel. During the hours of operation, $0 \le t \le 8$, the plant processes gravel at a constant rate of 100 tons per hour.

parts.prompt: Find $G'(5)$. Using correct units, interpret your answer in the context of the problem.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct value: $G'(5) \approx -24.588$ (or $-24.587$) tons per hour per hour
1 pt: Correct interpretation with units: the rate at which gravel is arriving is decreasing by approximately 24.588 tons per hour per hour at time $t = 5$ hours
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $G'(5) \approx -24.588$ tons/hr$^2$. Interpretation must reference rate of change of arrival rate, direction (decreasing), units, and context.

parts.prompt: Find the total amount of unprocessed gravel that arrives at the plant during the hours of operation on this workday.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct answer: $\int_0^8 G(t)\, dt \approx 825.551$ tons
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int_0^8 G(t)\, dt \approx 825.551$ tons of unprocessed gravel arrive.

parts.prompt: Is the amount of unprocessed gravel at the plant increasing or decreasing at time $t = 5$ hours? Show the work that leads to your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct conclusion: at $t = 5$, the rate of arrival ($G(5) \approx 98.141$) is less than the rate of processing (100 tons/hr), so the amount is decreasing
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $G(5) \approx 98.141 < 100$. Since arrival rate $<$ processing rate, amount is decreasing at $t = 5$.

parts.prompt: What is the maximum amount of unprocessed gravel at the plant during the hours of operation on this workday? Justify your answer.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Sets $A'(t) = G(t) - 100 = 0$ to find critical points: $t \approx 4.923$
1 pt: Correct maximum value: evaluates $A(t) = 500 + \int_0^t (G(s)-100)\, ds$ at candidates; $A(0)=500$, $A(4.923)\approx 635.376$, $A(8)\approx 525.551$; maximum is $\approx 635.376$ tons
1 pt: Justification: compares values at critical point and endpoints
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $A'(t) = G(t) - 100 = 0 \Rightarrow t \approx 4.923$. $A(4.923) \approx 635.376$ tons is the maximum (checked against $A(0) = 500$ and $A(8) \approx 525.551$).
